description_long: This house features a weathered, gable roof with aged, brownish-gray tiles and a slightly uneven surface, suggesting years of exposure to the elements. The exterior walls are finished in a faded, off-white stucco with visible patches of discoloration and wear, giving the building a rustic, lived-in appearance. Multiple rectangular windows with white frames are symmetrically arranged on the front and sides, while the rear includes a prominent entryway with a single wooden door, partially open and accessed by a short stone step. Copper-colored downspouts run along the corners, channeling rainwater from the roof. A small brick chimney protrudes from the roof, and a secondary, lower roof section extends from one side, adding architectural interest. The overall structure is compact, with a simple, functional design typical of early 20th-century European rural or suburban homes.
description: A weathered, gable-roofed house with faded stucco walls, symmetrical windows, and a prominent front entryway. The structure features rustic details like a brick chimney, copper downspouts, and a partially open wooden door.
description_view_0: The gable end shows two windows and visible copper downspouts, with the roof's peak and chimney partially visible.
description_view_1: The main facade displays a symmetrical arrangement of six windows and a small attic window, with the full width of the tiled roof and both downspouts in view.
description_view_2: The opposite gable end mirrors the first, with two windows and a clear view of the secondary roof section extending from the side.
description_view_3: The rear view highlights the main entryway with a partially open wooden door, flanked by two windows and accessed by a stone step.
attribute_entryway_design: Single wooden door with stone step, no porch, simple and functional
